name: Community Enterprise Investments, Incorporated
description_short: HUD-certified non-profit housing counselor and community development organization offering free credit counseling, homebuyer education, and affordable housing solutions in Florida.
description_long:
Community Enterprise Investments, Inc. (CEII) is a 501(c)(3) non-profit Community Development Corporation and Community Development Financial Institution (CDFI) based in the Escambia and Santa Rosa County areas of Florida. The organization has established itself as a trusted resource for underserved communities seeking financial stability and homeownership opportunities. CEII operates as a HUD-certified housing counseling agency, which means its staff meets federal standards for providing legitimate financial guidance without predatory practices or hidden fees.

CEII offers six core service areas: free HUD-certified housing counseling covering buying, renting, default, foreclosure avoidance, and credit issues; pre-homebuyer education courses available both online and in-person; credit counseling for debt management and financial recovery; affordable housing properties (apartments and single-family homes) scattered throughout Escambia and Santa Rosa Counties with affordable monthly rents; SBA microloan facilitation for small business development (businesses with fewer than five employees seeking up to $50,000); and general homeownership education. The organization positions itself as a comprehensive financial wellness provider rather than a single-solution lender.

What distinguishes CEII is its dual focus on housing and business development within an explicitly non-profit structure. Unlike for-profit credit counseling services, CEII's status as a 501(c)(3) and CDFI means it operates under stricter regulatory oversight and community benefit requirements. The organization offers HUD-certified counseling, which carries federal credibility and is often required by mortgage lenders before loan approval. The availability of in-person classes and direct phone support (850-595-6234, x206) suggests genuine local presence rather than purely digital operations. Their affordable housing portfolio demonstrates commitment beyond advisory services.

CEII appears genuinely positioned to help consumers, though limitations exist. The organization is geographically limited to Escambia and Santa Rosa Counties in Florida, restricting accessibility for consumers outside these areas. Website content lacks specific pricing details, program timelines, or detailed success metrics. The credit counseling description is brief and lacks granular information about debt payoff strategies, whether they offer debt management plans (DMPs), or typical counselor expertise. While HUD certification is valuable, the organization's capacity and wait times are not disclosed.
pros: HUD-certified housing counseling agency meeting federal standards for legitimate guidance
Free credit counseling and financial advice at no cost to consumers
Non-profit 501(c)(3) status with CDFI designation ensures regulatory oversight and community benefit requirements
Pre-homebuyer education available both online and in-person, accommodating different learning preferences
SBA microloan facilitation specifically designed for underbanked small business owners with fewer than 5 employees
Affordable housing portfolio with scattered properties reducing neighborhood segregation effects
Direct phone support and local presence in Escambia and Santa Rosa Counties
cons: Geographic limitation to Escambia and Santa Rosa Counties in Florida only
Website lacks specific pricing, program timelines, or measurable success metrics for credit counseling outcomes
Credit counseling description is minimal with no detail on debt management plan (DMP) options or counselor qualifications
No information about appointment availability, typical wait times, or capacity constraints
Limited detail on SBA microloan terms, interest rates, or approval timeline
best_for: Florida residents in Escambia/Santa Rosa Counties seeking free HUD-certified housing counseling before mortgage pre-approval
First-time homebuyers wanting non-predatory education about the home purchase process
Small business owners with fewer than 5 employees needing SBA microloans up to $50,000
Renters or homeowners facing default, foreclosure, or credit issues seeking legitimate free advice
diagnosis: CEII is best for Florida residents in Escambia and Santa Rosa Counties seeking legitimate, free HUD-certified housing counseling, first-time homebuyer education, or small business microloans. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation—out-of-state consumers cannot access services, and even in-state consumers outside these two counties are excluded.
services: HUD-certified housing counseling covering homebuying, renting, default, and foreclosure avoidance
Credit counseling and personalized debt management advice
Pre-homebuyer education courses (online and in-person formats)
Homeownership education and clarification on home buying process
Affordable rental apartments in Escambia and Santa Rosa Counties
Affordable single-family homes for rent with affordable monthly payments
SBA microloan facilitation for small business startups and existing businesses
Small business development guidance for businesses with fewer than five employees
Free financial advice and counseling to clients in default or foreclosure
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
